summarization.a: The image panel consists of series of ccanning electron microscopy (SEM) images of InGaAs/InAlAs vertical nanowires (VNWs) during different stages of HF/DMAC ALE process at 250 °C
summarization.b: The image panel consists of series of ccanning electron microscopy (SEM) image at different ALE stages at  300 °C
summarization.c: The line chart shows the change in X-ray reflectivity intensity with respect to omega-2θ (arcsec) for different cycles of ALE.
summarization.d: The line chart illustrates the change in radius (ΔRadius) over the number of ALE cycles for InAlAs and InGaAs at different temperatures.

| 300 cycles      | 24 nm            | 18 nm            |

d.question: Why is there a substantial difference in etching rate between InGaAs and InAlAs?
d.answer: The partial etching selectivity between InGaAs and InAlAs  might occur because fluorination of AlAs (Gibbs free energy ΔG° = −86.0 kcal/mol) is thermochemically more favorable than the fluorination of GaAs (ΔG° = −17.4 kcal/mol) at 250 °C. This behavior is also consistent with the faster oxidation of InAlAs versus InGaAs at 300−500 °C.Fluorination/oxidation of InAlAs is probably more favorable than for InGaAs because Al−F/Al-O bonds are stronger than Ga−F/Ga-O bonds.

b.question: How  well is the ALE of InGaAs at higher temperature?
b.answer:
The radial etch rates of InGaAs and InAlAs VNWs at 300 °C are as follows. 
1. After the first 250 cycles, InGaAs is 0.24 and InAlAs is 0.62 Å/cycle on average. 
2. After the second 250 cycles,  InGaAs  is 0.10 and InAlAs decreases to 0.41 Å/cycle on average.

a.question: Is the thermal ALE tested with fin and vertical nanowire (VNW) sidewalls before integrating the ALE process with a full transistor process?
Why the etching of InGaAs VNWs nearly stopped but etching of InAlAs continued?
a.answer: The negligible etching of InGaAs after 300 cycles of thermal ALE at 250 °C may be caused by the low temperature of 250 °C.
